page: Amazon Web Services t2.medium is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with an i386 CPU architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance features 2 physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and 1.0 thread per core. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 45 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the Xen hypervisor, includes no local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the t2.medium in the lower performance tiers for intensive workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng yield scores of 681.79, placing it in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show 153.48 GB/sec for small cached blocks, but drop to 17.58 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 12.64 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Geekbench scores reflect this trend, with a single-core score of 852.0 and a multi-core score of 1519.0. However, the instance achieves an average rating in text generation using a small 135M LLM model, reaching 72.31 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model registers at 209.88 tokens/sec.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and lack of bundled local storage or dedicated GPUs, the t2.medium is structured as a budget-friendly option for workloads with variable CPU utilization. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-resource tasks that do not require sustained high performance. Tradeoffs include poor multi-threaded decompression and database operation benchmarks, as shown by a Passmark database score of 1366.59. This instance is appropriate for light web serving, small-scale Redis caching, development environments, and low-throughput background tasks rather than sustained compute-heavy or memory-intensive production databases.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.medium is a burstable performance instance in the t2 family, running on the Xen hypervisor with an i386 CPU architecture. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, it features 2 physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate poor performance for sustained compute tasks, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 10%, though it maintains average performance for small-model LLM text generation at 72.31 tokens/sec. The t2.medium offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads with bursty CPU demands, making it suitable for light web serving, development environments, and low-intensity background processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.medium is a burstable Xen-based instance featuring a 2-core Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for low-cost, variable workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.medium is a burstable instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, suited for light, non-sustained workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly burstable instance with dual vCPUs and balanced memory for low-intensity or variable development workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 2 vCPUs.
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage.
Burstable CPU allocation designed for variable, non-sustained workloads.
Delivers 72.31 tokens/sec text generation for small LLM models.
Appropriate for light web serving, development environments, and basic testing.
